values.title: Liberty Under Law
values.summary: Freedom is not the absence of rules. It is the presence of just rules, applied equally, that protect each person from arbitrary power.

values.title: Civic Duty
values.summary: Citizenship is not a status. It is a set of obligations — to your neighbors, your community, and the institutions that make a free life possible.

values.title: Equal Dignity
values.summary: Every human being possesses an inherent worth that does not depend on usefulness, status, or agreement. Rights flow from this.

values.title: Limited Government
values.summary: Power concentrated is power abused. Separation of powers, federalism, and enumerated rights exist to keep authority answerable to the people.

values.title: Rule of Law
values.source: Common to every durable free society
values.summary: The same law applies to the powerful and the powerless. Without that, every other right is conditional.

values.title: Personal Responsibility
values.summary: A free society is built by free people who take responsibility for their families, their work, and their communities — and expect the same of others.

values.title: Pursuit of Truth
values.summary: Honest inquiry, open argument, and the willingness to be corrected are the foundation of both wisdom and self-government.

values.title: Service and Sacrifice
values.summary: What we inherit was paid for by people we will never meet. We owe the next generation the same.
